title: Slack Channel
description: A Slack conversation object representing a channel, direct message, multi-person direct message, or group conversation. This is the unified model returned by the conversations.* family of Web API methods, which consolidated the legacy channels.*, groups.*, im.*, and mpim.* endpoints. The type of conversation is identified by boolean flags (is_channel, is_group, is_im, is_mpim) and the is_private flag. Channels are uniquely identified by their id field, with public channels starting with C, private channels and multi-person DMs starting with G, and direct messages starting with D.

id.description: The unique identifier for this conversation. Public channels begin with C, private channels and multi-person DMs begin with G, and direct messages begin with D.

name.description: The name of the channel without the leading # sign. Channel names can contain lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores, with a maximum length of 80 characters. Not present for direct messages.

name_normalized.description: A normalized, lowercase version of the channel name. Useful for case-insensitive comparisons and sorting.

is_channel.description: Whether this conversation is a public channel. Public channels are visible to all members of the workspace and can be joined by anyone.

is_group.description: Whether this conversation is a private channel. Private channels are invite-only and not visible in the channel browser.

is_im.description: Whether this conversation is a direct message between two people.

is_mpim.description: Whether this conversation is a multi-person direct message. MPIMs are group DMs between 3 or more people.

is_private.description: Whether this conversation is private. True for private channels, DMs, and MPIMs. False for public channels.

is_archived.description: Whether the channel is archived. Archived channels are read-only and do not accept new messages. They can be unarchived by workspace admins.

is_general.description: Whether this is the workspace's #general channel. Every workspace has exactly one general channel that all members are automatically added to.

is_shared.description: Whether the channel is shared with another workspace using Slack Connect.

is_ext_shared.description: Whether the channel is shared with an external organization via Slack Connect. External shared channels allow cross-organization collaboration.

is_org_shared.description: Whether the channel is shared across the entire Enterprise Grid organization. Org-wide channels are available to all workspaces in the grid.

is_pending_ext_shared.description: Whether there is a pending Slack Connect invitation for this channel that has not yet been accepted.

is_member.description: Whether the authenticated user or bot is a member of this conversation. Users must be members to read history or post messages in private channels.

is_open.description: Whether the direct message or MPIM is currently open in the user's sidebar. Only relevant for DMs and MPIMs.

is_frozen.description: Whether the channel is frozen. Frozen channels do not accept new messages.

is_read_only.description: Whether the channel is read-only. In read-only channels, only admins and approved users can post.

is_thread_only.description: Whether the channel only allows threaded replies. Top-level posts are not permitted.

is_non_threadable.description: Whether threading is disabled in this channel.

created.description: Unix timestamp (in seconds) of when the conversation was created.

creator.description: The user ID of the member who created this channel. Not present for DMs.

updated.description: Unix timestamp of the last update to the conversation metadata (not the last message).

unlinked.description: The number of times this shared channel has been unlinked from a partner workspace.

topic.description: The channel's topic. Topics appear below the channel name in the Slack client and help describe what the channel is currently being used for.

value.description: The topic text. Supports mrkdwn formatting. Maximum 250 characters.

creator.description: The user ID of the person who last set the topic.

last_set.description: Unix timestamp of when the topic was last set. 0 if never set.

purpose.description: The channel's purpose. Purposes describe the intended use of the channel and help users decide whether to join.

value.description: The purpose text. Supports mrkdwn formatting. Maximum 250 characters.

creator.description: The user ID of the person who last set the purpose.

last_set.description: Unix timestamp of when the purpose was last set. 0 if never set.

previous_names.description: A list of all previous names this channel has had. Useful for tracking channel history and identifying channels that have been renamed.

num_members.description: The approximate number of members in the channel. Only included in responses when explicitly requested via the include_num_members parameter. May not be perfectly accurate for large channels.

locale.description: The IETF language code for the channel's locale. Only included in responses when explicitly requested via the include_locale parameter.

user.description: For direct messages, the user ID of the other participant. Not present for channels, groups, or MPIMs.

shared_team_ids.description: An array of team (workspace) IDs that share this channel. Present for shared and Slack Connect channels.

pending_shared.description: An array of team IDs for pending shared channel invitations.

pending_connected_team_ids.description: An array of team IDs for pending Slack Connect invitations.

parent_conversation.description: The parent conversation ID if this is a sub-conversation or thread-only channel. Null for top-level conversations.

context_team_id.description: The team (workspace) ID providing context for this conversation. Useful in Enterprise Grid environments where channels may be shared across workspaces.

properties.description: Additional channel properties including canvas and tab configurations.

tabs.description: An array of tab objects associated with the channel.

last_read.description: The timestamp of the last message read by the authenticated user in this channel.

latest.description: The most recent message in the channel. Only included in some API responses.

unread_count.description: The number of unread messages the authenticated user has in this channel.

unread_count_display.description: The number of unread messages to display (may differ from unread_count based on notification settings).

priority.description: A priority value used for sorting channels in the sidebar.
